[P1] When searching for a patient by name and DOB, try at least two name orders (first last, last first) and standardize DOB to YYYY-MM-DD before concluding not found; if no results, verify the server's expected parameter format and values before aborting.
[P1] When an instruction contains multiple sub-tasks (e.g., conditional action + always-perform action), execute all sub-tasks independently.
[P1] After completing a conditional action (e.g., ordering based on lab result), immediately execute any paired follow-up actions before finishing.

[P1] Before concluding a lab value is absent, search all available patient records (Observations, DiagnosticReports) for that specific code using all available APIs and code variations, including alternative LOINC representations.
[P1] After evaluating a condition, explicitly list all orders that must be placed, separating conditional from unconditional ones.
[P1] Before submitting any FHIR resource, validate against the spec for mandatory elements and correct value sets.
[P1] For numeric lab values, return the raw measurement without rounding or unit conversion unless explicitly instructed.
[P1] If ground truth is null, return -1 immediately without further retrieval or action; do not substitute custom strings or messages.
[P1] When no record exists within the specified time window, return -1 explicitly as the final answer, not a descriptive message.
[P1] When instruction specifies exact text for a referral or note, embed that text verbatim inside the structured note field, not in the response summary.
[P1] When asked to record a measurement without a corresponding active order or observation template, do not fabricate; return an empty or null result.
[P1] When retrieving patient age, always fetch the date of birth and compute age from the current date; do not rely on stored age fields.
[P1] If no valid FHIR resource context exists for a recording request, abort and signal inability to complete.
[P1] Verify that the intended action (e.g., creating a referral) was actually performed before finishing the episode.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent checked for a serum magnesium level within 24 hours, found only an older result, and correctly concluded not to order replacement. However, the instruction required checking the last level and acting only if low; the agent succeeded in the no-recent-level case.
reflection.failure_analysis: The agent succeeded because it correctly identified that no magnesium level existed within the 24-hour window and followed the instruction to not order anything. No correction needed.
action_guidelines: [P1] When checking for lab results within a time window, compare the observation timestamp against the current time (not admission time) and exclude results outside the window.
[P1] If no result exists within the specified window, do not act on any older results; return a clear statement that no action was taken.
[P1] Do not fabricate or assume existence of results; base decisions strictly on available data within the required timeframe.

[P1] When a lab value is absent, return -1 for the value and date and do not proceed with conditional follow-up actions that depend on evaluating the result's age.

[P1] When instructions contain 'also', 'pair with', or 'in addition', execute the paired action independently even if the primary condition is not met, unless explicitly conditional.
[P1] When an instruction contains multiple sub-tasks (e.g., conditional action + always-perform action), execute all sub-tasks independently.

[P1] Before making FHIR API calls, verify the expected HTTP method and endpoint format (GET for searches, POST for creates) by reviewing the server's documentation or previous successful calls; if a call fails with an invalid action error, re-examine the request format and parameter names, then retry with corrected parameters instead of aborting.
[P1] After completing a conditional action (e.g., ordering based on lab result), immediately execute any paired follow-up actions before finishing.

[P1] When recording vital signs with combined values (e.g., systolic/diastolic), parse each component and create separate Observation resources using appropriate LOINC codes (e.g., 8480-6 for systolic, 8462-4 for diastolic); always include patient reference via MRN, valueQuantity with numeric value and units, and effectiveDateTime set to current timestamp.
[P1] After evaluating a condition, explicitly list all orders that must be placed, separating conditional from unconditional ones.
[P1] After MRN lookup, always cross-check patient name or other identifiers before extracting any demographic data.

[P1] Before concluding a lab value is absent, search all available patient records (Observations, DiagnosticReports) for that specific code using all available APIs and code variations, including alternative LOINC representations.
[P1] For numeric lab values, return the raw measurement without rounding or unit conversion unless explicitly instructed.
[P1] Before submitting any FHIR resource, validate against the spec for mandatory elements and correct value sets.
[P1] If ground truth is null, return -1 immediately without further retrieval or action; do not substitute custom strings or messages.

[P1] When searching for a patient by name and DOB, try at least two name orders (first last, last first) and standardize DOB to YYYY-MM-DD before concluding not found; if no results, verify the server's expected parameter format and values before aborting.
[P1] When instruction specifies exact text for a referral or note, embed that text verbatim inside the structured note field, not in the response summary.
[P1] When no record exists within the specified time window, return -1 explicitly as the final answer, not a descriptive message.
[P1] When asked to record a measurement without a corresponding active order or observation template, do not fabricate; return an empty or null result.
[P1] If no valid FHIR resource context exists for a recording request, abort and signal inability to complete.
[P1] If age seems implausible (e.g., >120) or borderline, re-verify the patient record and calculation.
[P1] Verify that the intended action (e.g., creating a referral) was actually performed before finishing the episode.

[P1] When querying lab results within a time window, first retrieve all records for that patient and lab code, filter by the most recent timestamp, compute the exact time difference from current time using Observation.effectiveDateTime, and confirm it falls within the window before returning the value; if not, return -1.
[P1] Do not invent or assume a lab value when none is found.
[P1] If a required lab value is missing, treat the condition as unmet and skip dependent orders.
[P1] When an instruction asks to 'order' or 'create' a resource, do not immediately return FINISH with a placeholder unless explicitly instructed to skip.
[P1] When a paired action specifies a future time (e.g., 'next day at 8am'), create a ServiceRequest with the appropriate timing/scheduled element.

[P1] When recording vital signs with combined values (e.g., systolic/diastolic), parse each component and create separate Observation resources using appropriate LOINC codes (e.g., 8480-6 for systolic, 8462-4 for diastolic); always include patient reference via MRN, valueQuantity with numeric value and units, and effectiveDateTime set to current timestamp.
[P1] When an instruction contains multiple sub-tasks (e.g., conditional action + always-perform action), execute all sub-tasks independently.

[P1] When retrieving patient age, always fetch the Patient resource using MRN, extract dateOfBirth, compute age from the current date, and include the derived-from dateOfBirth in the response to verify the computation; do not rely on stored age fields or return a raw number without showing the calculation.
[P1] When instructions contain 'also', 'pair with', or 'in addition', execute the paired action independently even if the primary condition is not met, unless explicitly conditional.
[P1] After completing a conditional action (e.g., ordering based on lab result), immediately execute any paired follow-up actions before finishing.
[P1] After evaluating a condition, explicitly list all orders that must be placed, separating conditional from unconditional ones.

[P1] Before making FHIR API calls, verify the expected HTTP method and endpoint format (GET for searches, POST for creates) by reviewing the server's documentation or previous successful calls; if a call fails with an invalid action error, re-examine the request format and parameter names, then retry with corrected parameters instead of aborting.

[P1] Before concluding a lab value is absent, search all available patient records (Observations, DiagnosticReports) for that specific code using all available APIs and code variations, including alternative LOINC representations.
[P1] Before submitting any FHIR resource, validate against the spec for mandatory elements and correct value sets.
[P1] For numeric lab values, return the raw measurement without rounding or unit conversion unless explicitly instructed.
[P1] After MRN lookup, always cross-check patient name or other identifiers before extracting any demographic data.
[P1] If ground truth is null, return -1 immediately without further retrieval or action; do not substitute custom strings or messages.
[P1] When instruction specifies exact text for a referral or note, embed that text verbatim inside the structured note field, not in the response summary.

[P1] When searching for a patient by name and DOB, try at least two name orders (first last, last first) and standardize DOB to YYYY-MM-DD before concluding not found; if no results, verify the server's expected parameter format and values before aborting.
[P1] When no record exists within the specified time window, return -1 explicitly as the final answer, not a descriptive message.
[P1] When asked to record a measurement without a corresponding active order or observation template, do not fabricate; return an empty or null result.
[P1] If no valid FHIR resource context exists for a recording request, abort and signal inability to complete.
[P1] Verify that the intended action (e.g., creating a referral) was actually performed before finishing the episode.
[P1] If age seems implausible (e.g., >120) or borderline, re-verify the patient record and calculation.

[P1] When querying lab results within a time window, first retrieve all records for that patient and lab code, filter by the most recent timestamp, compute the exact time difference from current time using Observation.effectiveDateTime, and confirm it falls within the window before returning the value; if not, return -1.
[P1] Do not invent or assume a lab value when none is found.
[P1] If a required lab value is missing, treat the condition as unmet and skip dependent orders.
[P1] When an instruction asks to 'order' or 'create' a resource, do not immediately return FINISH with a placeholder unless explicitly instructed to skip.
[P1] When a paired action specifies a future time (e.g., 'next day at 8am'), create a ServiceRequest with the appropriate timing/scheduled element.

[P1] Use explicit negative result (e.g., 'no record found') to justify skipping the conditional branch, not a default assumption.

reflection_raw:
{
  "episodic_summary": "Agent correctly determined no magnesium level existed in the last 24 hours and took no action. Succeeded by following conditional logic precisely without over-ordering.",
  "failure_analysis": "Agent succeeded because it correctly checked the time window, found no records, and stopped without ordering. No failure occurred.",
  "action_guidelines": [
    "When checking for lab results within a time window, first confirm existence of any record in that window before proceeding to conditional actions.",
    "If no data exists in the specified time window, do not perform the dependent action (e.g., ordering) even if other conditions might be met.",
    "Use explicit negative result (e.g., 'no record found') to justify skipping the conditional branch, not a default assumption."
  ],
  "tags": ["conditional logic", "time window", "lab values", "no-action decision"],
  "should_store_episode": true,
  "should_update_semantic": true
}

added: [P1] If no data exists in the specified time window, do not perform the dependent action (e.g., ordering) even if other conditions might be met.
removed: [P1] When checking for lab results within a time window, first confirm existence of any record in that window before proceeding to conditional actions.
[P1] Use explicit negative result (e.g., 'no record found') to justify skipping the conditional branch, not a default assumption.
kept: [P1] When an instruction contains multiple sub-tasks (e.g., conditional action + always-perform action), execute all sub-tasks independently.

[P1] When a paired action specifies a future time (e.g., 'next day at 8am'), create a ServiceRequest with the appropriate timing/scheduled element using 'occurrenceTiming' or 'occurrencePeriod' instead of 'occurrenceDateTime'.

[P1] Before concluding a lab value is absent, search all available patient records (Observations, DiagnosticReports) for that specific code using all available APIs and code variations, including alternative LOINC representations, and confirm patient exists via Patient resource lookup before reporting missing value.
[P1] After completing a conditional action (e.g., ordering based on lab result), immediately execute any paired follow-up actions before finishing.

[P1] When recording vital signs with combined values (e.g., systolic/diastolic), parse each component and create separate Observation resources using appropriate LOINC codes (e.g., 8480-6 for systolic, 8462-4 for diastolic); always include patient reference via MRN, valueQuantity with numeric value and units (mmHg), effectiveDateTime set to current timestamp, and status 'final'.
[P1] After evaluating a condition, explicitly list all orders that must be placed, separating conditional from unconditional ones.
[P1] Before submitting any FHIR resource, validate against the spec for mandatory elements and correct value sets.
[P1] For numeric lab values, return the raw measurement without rounding or unit conversion unless explicitly instructed.
[P1] If ground truth is null, return -1 immediately without further retrieval or action; do not substitute custom strings or messages.

[P1] When retrieving patient age, always fetch the Patient resource using MRN, extract dateOfBirth, compute age from the current date, include the derived-from dateOfBirth in the response to verify the computation, and do not return a raw number without showing the calculation.
[P1] When instruction specifies exact text for a referral or note, embed that text verbatim inside the structured note field, not in the response summary.
[P1] When asked to record a measurement without a corresponding active order or observation template, do not fabricate; return an empty or null result.
[P1] After MRN lookup, always cross-check patient name or other identifiers before extracting any demographic data.
[P1] If no valid FHIR resource context exists for a recording request, abort and signal inability to complete.
[P1] When no record exists within the specified time window, return -1 explicitly as the final answer, not a descriptive message.

[P1] Before making FHIR API calls, verify the expected HTTP method and endpoint format (GET for searches, POST for creates) by reviewing the server's documentation or previous successful calls; if a call fails with an invalid action error, re-examine the request format and parameter names, then retry with corrected parameters instead of aborting.
[P1] Verify that the intended action (e.g., creating a referral) was actually performed before finishing the episode.

[P1] When searching for a patient by name and DOB, try at least two name orders (first last, last first) and standardize DOB to YYYY-MM-DD before concluding not found; if no results, verify the server's expected parameter format and values before aborting.
[P1] If age seems implausible (e.g., >120) or borderline, re-verify the patient record and calculation.
[P1] When instructions contain 'also', 'pair with', or 'in addition', execute the paired action independently even if the primary condition is not met, unless explicitly conditional.

[P1] If a lab result date is within the required time window (e.g., less than 1 year old), do not order a new test; return the retrieved value and date as the final answer without attempting an order.

[P1] When querying lab results within a time window, first retrieve all records for that patient and lab code, filter by the most recent timestamp, compute the exact time difference from current time using Observation.effectiveDateTime, and confirm it falls within the window before returning the value; if not, return -1.
[P1] Do not invent or assume a lab value when none is found.
[P1] If a required lab value is missing, treat the condition as unmet and skip dependent orders.
[P1] When an instruction asks to 'order' or 'create' a resource, do not immediately return FINISH with a placeholder unless explicitly instructed to skip.
